claim: Ralph Wiggum's real value is not vibe coding but rather as a structured framework for building proof-of-concepts quickly with proper planning built in
actionable: Use Ralph Wiggum loops to validate architecture, tech stack, and features in proof-of-concept phases before committing to production implementation.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: A properly structured Ralph Wiggum loop requires three key files: a PRD (product requirements document) with clearly defined tasks, a prompt.md with context, and an activity.md for long-term memory across iterations
actionable: Set up a structured Ralph loop by creating PRD with feature tasks, prompt.md with full context, and activity.md to track completion across iterations.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: The only valid exit condition for a Ralph Wiggum loop is when all features in the PRD are marked as complete/passing - otherwise the loop runs indefinitely or until max iterations
actionable: Define clear completion tokens and completion criteria upfront; only exit the loop when all features pass validation.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: Ralph Wiggum loops should include self-validation steps where the agent tests its own work (using tools like Vercel Agent Browser) before moving to the next iteration
actionable: Incorporate browser automation or testing tools into your Ralph loop so agents can validate their own implementations end-to-end before completing features.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: Cloud Code hooks (sandbox mode) are essential for running Ralph loops - they limit what commands the agent can execute, preventing catastrophic damage
actionable: Always configure settings.json with permissions and restricted command lists before running long-running agentic loops like Ralph.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: Keep the PRD scope tightly defined and validated before starting the Ralph loop - harnesses don't work well without clearly defined goals and feature lists
actionable: Create a tightly scoped PRD with 15-25 specific, validatable features before kicking off a Ralph Wiggum loop.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: AI coding assistants can build multiple complete proof-of-concept versions of an application end-to-end in hours, which enables rapid validation of architecture and tech stack decisions
actionable: Use AI coding agents to rapidly prototype multiple versions or variations to test different architectural approaches.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum

claim: Even with AI agents, you still need to maintain driver's seat control by creating the plan yourself - have the agent walk through discovery but you finalize the tech stack and feature list
actionable: Stay in the driver's seat during planning; let agents make recommendations but you make final decisions on architecture and scope.
source_title: I Was Wrong About Ralph Wiggum
